stem: A cube has an edge length of 23 inches. What is the volume, in cubic inches, of the cube?
rationale: Choice B is correct. The volume V of a cube with edge length s is V = s3. Substituting s = 23 yields V = 233 = 12,167 cubic inches. Choice A is the area of one face. Choice C corresponds to 6 times the area of a face (surface area). Choice D is three times the edge length.
